title: ATDD acceptance tests bypass the MCP protocol public interface
evidence: test/brazilian_soccer_mcp/acceptance_test.clj:30 calls tools/find-matches directly (and tools/get-team-stats, tools/find-players, etc.) instead of driving core/handle-request with a JSON-RPC tools/call message; core.clj:100-125 (handle-request dispatch, keywordize-args, tool-schemas, JSON serialization, error handling) has zero test coverage. The ATDD prompt requires exercising the SUT 'only through its public interface (here, the MCP tools/protocol) with no back-door access to internals'.
suggestion: Add at least one acceptance test that sends a JSON-RPC tools/call map through core/handle-request and asserts on the MCP content response, so the actual protocol surface is verified.

title: Acceptance scenarios share a preloaded full dataset rather than starting empty/independent
evidence: test/brazilian_soccer_mcp/acceptance_test.clj:18-22 uses a single `defonce loaded-data` (delay over the real data/kaggle CSVs) shared across all 20 acceptance tests; assertions depend on real dataset contents (e.g. line 242 'Flamengo won 2019', line 144 '>100 Brazilian players'). The ATDD prompt requires each scenario to 'start from a running but empty system and share no data with other tests'.
suggestion: Drive each scenario from a small fixture dataset built per-test (or document why the real corpus is used) so scenarios are atomic and independent.

title: Duplicate condition in normalize-br-competition
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer_mcp/data.clj:96 — `(or (str/includes? t "copa do brasil") (str/includes? t "copa do brasil"))` tests the same substring twice; the second clause is dead code (likely a copy-paste of a missing alias).
suggestion: Remove the duplicate or replace the second clause with the intended alias (e.g. "copa").

title: Extra match statistics captured beyond spec
evidence: src/brazilian_soccer_mcp/data.clj:112-115 loads corners/shots from BR-Football-Dataset; tools.clj:283-343 exposes biggest-wins / goals-per-match / home-away-record / top-scoring-teams beyond the single aggregate stat required by R10.
suggestion: None — surfaced as an enhancement beyond the spec, not a deduction.
